narrative.headline: Brakes and electronics top the 2019 Altima complaint list
narrative.summary: Owners filed 221 complaints with NHTSA. The most reported area is the electrical system (24%), followed by the engine (20%) and the forward collision avoidance system (19%). Watch the safety systems closely: the forward collision feature is tied to 3 crashes, and the service brakes stand out with 9 crashes, 2 fires, and 3 injuries despite fewer reports. The engine had no crashes, fires, or injuries reported.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Most-reported area, with 54 complaints (24%). 5 led to a crash and 2 to injuries.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 44 complaints (20%) and the second most common, but with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 41 complaints (19%): owners report the system braking or slowing on its own with nothing ahead. Tied to 3 crashes.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Fewer reports (29, 13%) but the most dangerous: 9 crashes, 2 fires, and 3 injuries. The single area to watch most.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: 14 complaints (6%), the smallest group, with 2 injuries and no crashes or fires reported.
